name: Quân
description: Old World Bearded Vulture - Gypaetus barbatus

intro.speaker: You
intro.text: Hey… Uncle Quân…

intro.speaker: You
intro.text: How are–

intro.speaker: Quân
intro.text: Stop mumbling and ask the question.

intro.speaker: You
intro.text: How are you doing?

intro.speaker: You
intro.text: I haven’t seen you in a while.

intro.speaker: Quân
intro.text: I’m doing the same as always.

intro.speaker: Quân
intro.text: And you?

intro.speaker: Quân
intro.text: Still caught up on all that shit with Linh’s bastard ex?

intro.speaker: You
intro.text: …yes.

intro.speaker: You
intro.text: I’m still ‘caught up in all that shit’ with my father.

intro.speaker: You
intro.text: … who raised me for fifteen years.

intro.speaker: Quân
intro.text: Sure, he’s that too.

intro.speaker: Quân
intro.text: Never liked the guy myself, but whatever.

intro.speaker: You
intro.text: Thank you for the commentary.

intro.speaker: You
intro.text: There’s been a lot of hating my dad going around since he left.

intro.speaker: Quân
intro.text: Sounds like is the majority opinion,

intro.speaker: Quân
intro.text: Probably deserved, then.

intro.speaker: You
intro.text: What have you even got against him? He didn’t do anything to you.

intro.speaker: Quân
intro.text: I am well within my rights to shit-talk my sisters douche of an ex her ditched her and their fucking kids because of one fucking incident.

intro.speaker: You
intro.text: That’s true, but… I mean, mum is the one who cheated.

intro.speaker: Quân
intro.text: I honestly don’t give a fuck what my sister did or didn’t do.

intro.speaker: You
intro.text: I mean, morally…

intro.speaker: Quân
intro.text: Family is family, kid, and as far as I’m concerned, your father’s not part of it anymore. Of his own volition, even.

intro.speaker: Quân
intro.text: I’m definitely not going to be taking his side.

intro.speaker: Quân
intro.text: Never liked him anyway.

intro.speaker: You
intro.text: Rewriting history a bit, aren’t you?

intro.speaker: Quân
intro.text: I’m not a fan of these accusations, kid.

intro.speaker: Quân
intro.text: Do you have something to say to me?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You used FACTS AND LOGIC

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: Are you really getting on my case about ‘disrespecting you’ or something while you shit-talk my dad in front of me?

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: You seemed pretty quiet about all that to me growing up.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You used FACTS AND LOGIC

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: You don’t seem to share the same resentment for Lia’s mother for not being around.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: Is it different if they leave immediately as opposed to trying for fifteen years?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You used FACTS AND LOGIC

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: You’re a fucking hypocrit for holding dad accountable for his actions and then turning around and admitting flat out that you wouldn’t care if he was still family.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: Is it because he left? Or is it just because you seemed to have hated him anyway and just wanted an excuse?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You used BLATANT LIE

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: I’ve always thought you were kind of sketchy, you know?

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: You always glared and frowned at me as a child.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: Scared the shit out of me back then.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You used BLATANT LIE

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: I really hated mum for what she did.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: And you for taking her side so heavily that it seems like she didn’t have to introspect at all.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You used BLATANT LIE

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: I don't blame dad for leaving if this was really all he had here.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: I really don’t.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You used EMOTIONAL MANIPULATION

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It was super effective!

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: You’re talking pretty big for someone who barely showed up himself.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: You weren’t around when Louis dad died, you weren’t around when my dad left.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: If it’s so simple, then where were you?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You used EMOTIONAL MANIPULATION

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It was super effective!

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: I wanted to talk to you after it happened.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: But even before it all happened I barely saw you…

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: I barely saw Lia either…

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: You never visited properly. Doesn’t she even know anyone but you?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You used EMOTIONAL MANIPULATION

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It was super effective!

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: You say ‘family is family’, and you’re quick to criticise anyone you think is doing it wrong, but I’ll be honest, Quân…

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: You never have been particularly warm or easy to talk to.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: … especially not now.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Quân used INTIMIDATE

dialogue.speaker: Quân
dialogue.text: Spit out your fucking point already, kid.

dialogue.speaker: Quân
dialogue.text: You’re talking in circles.

dialogue.speaker: Quân
dialogue.text: Either say what you mean or don’t say anything at all.

dialogue.speaker: Quân
dialogue.text: That’s what I thought.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Quân used LORE DROP

dialogue.speaker: Quân
dialogue.text: When I was your age, this guy I knew tried to kill me.

dialogue.speaker: Quân
dialogue.text: I was helping him out with this thing – long story, not important – and when he finally didn’t need my help anymore, he tried to knife me.

dialogue.speaker: Quân
dialogue.text: unsuccessfully, obviously, he was pretty bad with a knife.

dialogue.speaker: Quân
dialogue.text: But the important this is that I learned that you need to be careful who you trust and how you let those people treat you.

dialogue.speaker: Quân
dialogue.text: They’re always circling, waiting for a weakness.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Quân used OH SHIT, REAL BIRD!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It was super effective!

dialogue.speaker: Quân
dialogue.text: Con kên kên

dialogue.speaker: Quân
dialogue.text: Get him.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: What?

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: Oh Jesus! BIRD! BIRD!

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: What the fuck!

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: I thought those were metaphorical!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Quân used JUDGEMENT

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It’s not very effective.

dialogue.speaker: Quân
dialogue.text: God damn, kid.

dialogue.speaker: Quân
dialogue.text: I never knew my expectations for someone could fall so… low.

intermission-50%.speaker: Quân
intermission-50%.text: Look, kid. What I don’t get is why you’re trying so hard to defend this guy.

intermission-50%.speaker: Quân
intermission-50%.text: He walked out on you.

intermission-50%.speaker: Quân
intermission-50%.text: You’re picking fights with me like I’m not one of the people that’s stuck around.

intermission-50%.speaker: Quân
intermission-50%.text: Which is a low fucking bar, but it’s what you’re measuring aginst at this point.

intermission-50%.speaker: Quân
intermission-50%.text: He wasn’t even that good to you when he was around.

intermission-50%.speaker: Quân
intermission-50%.text: Real piece of shit.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: I think you’re being a little harsh…

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: He wasn’t that bad.

intermission-50%.speaker: Quân
intermission-50%.text: He kind of was.

intermission-50%.speaker: Quân
intermission-50%.text: You were practically a fetus when he left–

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: I was fifteen.

intermission-50%.speaker: Quân
intermission-50%.text: And you’re barely any older now, but take it from a guy who actually saw him on even footing.

intermission-50%.speaker: Quân
intermission-50%.text: Your dad was a piece of work.

intermission-50%.speaker: Quân
intermission-50%.text: You must have been about one or two at the time, but there was a solid period of time when he tried to get Caspian to start calling him his name or fucking ‘sir’ instead of ‘dad’.

intermission-50%.speaker: Quân
intermission-50%.text: This is when Caspian was seven or so…

intermission-50%.speaker: Quân
intermission-50%.text: Something about him having a real kid now, or not wanting to differentiate you two.

intermission-50%.speaker: Quân
intermission-50%.text: … he was always fucking weird about that kid…

intermission-50%.speaker: Quân
intermission-50%.text: Screwed up Caspian at the time too. I remember Linh screaming her head off at the bastard when she found out.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: I… didn’t know about that.

intermission-50%.speaker: Quân
intermission-50%.text: You wouldn’t, Linh shut that shit down.

intermission-50%.speaker: Quân
intermission-50%.text: …heard from Caspian the bastard brought it up a few times when he was older too.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: I… thought they got along better than that.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: Dad always seemed to like Caspian way more than me…

intermission-50%.speaker: Quân
intermission-50%.text: See, that shit was a fucking nightmare too.

intermission-50%.speaker: Quân
intermission-50%.text: You can make up all these excuses about him doing it subconsciously, or not thinking it did any harm or something,

intermission-50%.speaker: Quân
intermission-50%.text: But that guy threw you two at each other like it was a blood sport.

intermission-50%.speaker: Quân
intermission-50%.text: And he wasn’t a fucking child, he had to have known what he was doing.

intermission-50%.speaker: Quân
intermission-50%.text: Especially after I pointed it out to the guy.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: I didn’t realise it was a big enough problem to get… pointed out.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: I… thought it was in my head…

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: I mean… he was just… he wanted me to be better at things, I suppose.

intermission-50%.speaker: Quân
intermission-50%.text: Of course you didn’t realise this stuff, you were an infant.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: Again, I was fifteen.

intermission-50%.speaker: Quân
intermission-50%.text: Whatever, same thing.

intermission-50%.speaker: Quân
intermission-50%.text: … Did you ever wonder why you spent so much more time with Thi Ha and Louis than you did with me and Lia?

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: Not really?

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: I assume it was just more convenient. Or that Thi Ha and mum were just closer.

intermission-50%.speaker: Quân
intermission-50%.text: Nah.

intermission-50%.speaker: Quân
intermission-50%.text: It’s because your ‘dad’ hated me.

intermission-50%.speaker: Quân
intermission-50%.text: … or he was scared of me, or something.

intermission-50%.speaker: Quân
intermission-50%.text: I may have punched him once or twice… he deserved it, though.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: That… checks out…

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: You are kind of… intimidating…

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: Though, I can’t really imagine dad being scared of anything, not enough to avoid you like that.

intermission-50%.speaker: Quân
intermission-50%.text: Let me tell you a little something about human relationships: It’s all about the give and take.

intermission-50%.speaker: Quân
intermission-50%.text: Funny thing is, for the most part, the whole thing is unequal.

intermission-50%.speaker: Quân
intermission-50%.text: What you’ve got to decide is what relationships are worth giving more of yourself to than you get in return.

intermission-50%.speaker: Quân
intermission-50%.text: Or which ones you need to defend yourself against.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: That’s… bleak.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: Wouldn’t you only want relationships that give you more back?

intermission-50%.speaker: Quân
intermission-50%.text: Sure.

intermission-50%.speaker: Quân
intermission-50%.text: That’s called being a selfish fucking bastard.

intermission-50%.speaker: Quân
intermission-50%.text: Like your dad.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: That felt unnecessarily pointed.

intermission-50%.speaker: Quân
intermission-50%.text: Did it? I wonder why.

win.speaker: Quân
win.text: You’re a good kid, y’know?

win.speaker: Quân
win.text: I mean, you’re stumbling through these conversations like a drunken baby giraffe that’s never had a real thought in its life, but…

win.speaker: Quân
win.text: Five years away from the family… it’d be easier to just drift off and run away from it all.

win.speaker: Quân
win.text: Takes some determination, if nothing else, to come back and try to make things work.

win.speaker: You
win.text: You could just say something nice.

win.speaker: You
win.text: The giraffe thing was unnecessary.

win.speaker: Quân
win.text: That’s why it’s so fucking sad that you’re still bleeding yourself dry making excuses for that bastard after all this time.

win.speaker: Quân
win.text: And not even because you’re trying to contact him…

win.speaker: Quân
win.text: You’re just waiting.

win.speaker: Quân
win.text: If you really wanted answers, kid… he’s not that hard to find.

win.speaker: You
win.text: I’ve tried to contact him. I’m not just sitting around.

win.speaker: You
win.text: He just… doesn’t answer…

win.speaker: You
win.text: But he hasn’t blocked me either.

win.speaker: You
win.text: I don’t know…

win.speaker: Quân
win.text: We’re in the fucking information age, kid.

win.speaker: Quân
win.text: Just stalking him to find where he is and show up in person.

win.speaker: Quân
win.text: That’s the only way you’re gonna get any answers, and if you need that to move one then go and get it.

win.speaker: You
win.text: He’s not gonna be happy to see me if I do that.

win.speaker: You
win.text: He hates confrontations.

win.speaker: Quân
win.text: So you are aware that he’s a coward.

win.speaker: You
win.text: That’s not what I said.

win.speaker: Quân
win.text: Fine, but it’s what I think.

win.speaker: Quân
win.text: The guy only ever deals with people he thinks are weaker than him.

win.speaker: Quân
win.text: Ones that he can tell are about to drop out of the fight any second.

win.speaker: Quân
win.text: That’s why he hated me, and that's why he barely fought for anything in the divorce with Linh.

win.speaker: Quân
win.text: He hates losing, so he doesn’t fight.

win.speaker: You
win.text: Everybody hates losing.

win.speaker: You
win.text: I hate losing, why do you think I keep getting into these stupid arguments.

win.speaker: You
win.text: It’s not exactly a revelation.

win.speaker: Quân
win.text: …Well, you’re not wrong.

win.speaker: Quân
win.text: Hell, I hate losing too…

win.speaker: Quân
win.text: But that’s why you’ve always got to go for fucking gold.

win.speaker: Quân
win.text: If you don’t try you’ve already lost… and once you start losing, it’s hard to stop.

win.speaker: Quân
win.text: People tend to kick you when you’re down.

win.speaker: You
win.text: You are a fountain of incredibly pessimistic advice.

win.speaker: You
win.text: I guess I get why Lia’s such a weirdo now.

win.speaker: Quân
win.text: There’s a life lesson I learned on the boat down to Australia after we left Vietnam, y’know?

win.speaker: Quân
win.text: Coming down the coast of Malaysia, needing to restock, but the Malaysian government's policy on boat people was to turn them away…

win.speaker: Quân
win.text: We got out supplies for the last leg of the journey from the Western tourists on a beach resort because we were a sad enough sight for them to pity us.

win.speaker: Quân
win.text: Literally begging for scraps…

win.speaker: You
win.text: You learned that people are actually kind and will help you if they can?

win.speaker: You
win.text: And that humanity has some inherent good in us?

win.speaker: Quân
win.text: I mean, yeah, sure, that too.

win.speaker: Quân
win.text: But I learned something about myself, and the people on that boat.

win.speaker: Quân
win.text: Inside even the most proud person, is a survivalist.

win.speaker: Quân
win.text: The people on that boat used to be well connected, affluent people before the fucking communists did their shit.

win.speaker: Quân
win.text: But when push comes to shove, they were prepared to let go of their pride.

win.speaker: You
win.text: You’re saying that like it’s a bad thing.

win.speaker: Quân
win.text: It's not…

win.speaker: Quân
win.text: Just that I’ve had an awareness, I suppose, of how much everyone has in them the capacity to take… and how to go for the weakest link.

win.speaker: You
win.text: Tourists?

win.speaker: Quân
win.text: They’re sure easier to convince than the government.

win.speaker: You
win.text: So… what does that have to do with me?

win.speaker: You
win.text: Or dad?

win.speaker: Quân
win.text: Just to be careful not to let him take advantage of you because you’re too sentimental for some version of him in your head that isn’t real.

win.speaker: You
win.text: … noted.

lose.speaker: Quân
lose.text: You are a stubborn, stupid kid aren’t you?

lose.speaker: Quân
lose.text: And here I thought Louis was gonna be the kid to worry about.

lose.speaker: Quân
lose.text: But at least Louis doesn’t come into the family get togethers swinging like you do.

lose.speaker: Quân
lose.text: You’re a real landmine of a kid, you know?

lose.speaker: You
lose.text: You’re the one who sicked a bird on me!

lose.speaker: Quân
lose.text: It just means I don’t know whether it’s really worth inviting you to these at all.

lose.speaker: Quân
lose.text: Now get out of my fucking face.
